experiment: Lever-2 derived-residue CONFIRM (N=8, real state.derived injection path)
substrate: Fork-A ledger repo + off-disk wire contract; held-out accept.py (anti-idiomatic, not inferable from pristine code)
mechanism: PRODUCTIONIZED path (differs from the screen's DERIVED.md proxy): the architect emits the contract into state.derived via `pigeon handoff --json-in`; coordinate._upstream_derived_markdown renders it as a '## Carried reasoning' markdown block injected into each downstream prompt. inject=1 verified on every with-derived trial.
design: Two physically separate worktrees (wt-bridge / wt-nobridge); schema synced to 1.2 (else state.derived is rejected); pristine-asserted per trial.
note: Trials 6-8 of the first batch hit a session rate-limit (api_error_status 429, turn-1 no-ops) and were discarded + re-run after the limit refreshed. The 8 reported trials are all valid real runs (22-26 turns, 130-155s).

pointers-only.failure_mode: idiomatic keys / ISO-string ts instead of the contract's acct/cents/ts epoch-int

verdict: GO — CONFIRMED. In the regime where the reasoning is genuinely irreducible, the carried derived residue is NECESSARY (8/8 vs 0/8; CIs cleanly separated, no overlap) and FREE (cheaper than the failing null: $0.417 vs $0.436). The panel's 're-derives cheaply' failure mode does NOT fire for a capable same-model receiver on a non-re-derivable constraint. This confirms the N=3 screen on the productionized injection path.
